business.headline: Why SK Hynix shares crashed over 15% days after their Nasdaq debutJust days after making a strong start in the US market, the South Korean chipmaker's shares plunged more than 15% on Monday, as investors rushed to book profits.

Delhi's property boom is creating an unusual courtroom problemWhat do soaring home prices have to do with courtrooms? According to the Delhi High Court, more than you might think. Here's the unusual problem now under scrutiny.

Why is crude oil back on the boil?Higher crude oil prices increase the country's import bill and can put pressure on inflation if fuel and transportation costs rise. They can also weaken the rupee as oil marketing companies require more US dollars to pay for imports.

Why TCS, Infosys and HCLTech shares rose despite a weak marketShares of Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), Infosys, HCLTech and LTIMindtree rose in early trade, outperforming the broader market and signalling renewed investor interest in a sector that has been under pressure for months.

What's behind India's tougher stance in trade talks with USThe US entered trade talks from a position of strength. But a series of developments may be tilting the balance towards New Delhi. Here's why India is in no hurry to strike a deal.

Sensex falls over 300 points: Why is the stock market falling today?Indian benchmark indices fell sharply after renewed US-Iran hostilities lifted crude oil concerns. The sell-off reflected worries over India's import bill, inflation and the market impact if Brent climbs above $90.

Sensex opens 600 points lower, Nifty tops 24,000; IndiGo down 3%The BSE Sensex fell 626.40 points, or 0.81%, to 76,942.99, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 184 points, or 0.76%, to 24,022.90 as of 9:25 am.

A joint loan, a separation and a damaged credit score: Her story is a warningShe wasn't the one who defaulted, yet her loan application was affected. He never borrowed the money, yet he had to repay the debt. These two real-life stories reveal the hidden risks of saying 'yes' to someone else's loan. Could the same happen to you?

Swiggy Instamart receives 9 notices from food regulator over quality complaintsFSSAI has issued nine notices to Swiggy Instamart over consumer complaints about expired, spoiled and contaminated food deliveries. The regulator has sought detailed compliance records and warned of legal action if the response is delayed.

Apple sues OpenAI, ex-employees over alleged trade secrets theftApple has sued OpenAI and two former employees, alleging misuse of trade secrets for consumer hardware. The case sharpens tensions between partners as both companies push deeper into AI devices.

RBI auctions Rs 32,000 crore in govt bonds: Are they among the safest investments?Government bonds are back in the spotlight after the RBI's latest Rs 32,000 crore auction. What makes them different from other investments, and who are they best suited for? Have a look.

8th Pay Commission: Why its impact goes far beyond government salariesEconomists say the 8th Pay Commission will affect far more than central government salaries and pensions. Its recommendations could shape consumer demand, state finances, inflation and fiscal planning.

File Early, Stay Relaxed: Why the Income Tax Department is sending SMS alertsThousands of taxpayers are receiving SMS reminders from the Income Tax Department asking them to file their ITR early. What's behind these messages?

Sensex ends 828 points higher, Nifty above 24,200; RIL up 2%The BSE Sensex surged 827.57 points, or 1.08%, to close at 77,569.39, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 244.10 points, or 1.02%, to settle at 24,206.90.

Why wait for the IPO? Some investors are buying shares much earlier. You can tooLong before an IPO opens, and sometimes even years before a company files its draft papers with the market regulator, its shares can already be bought and sold legally in what's known as the unlisted or pre-IPO market.

Income tax department launches ITR-7 Excel utility for AY 2026-27With the utility now live, eligible taxpayers can download it, fill in the required information offline, validate the details, generate a JSON file and upload it on the Income Tax e-Filing portal to complete the return filing process.

TCS's hiring surprise: Why India's biggest IT firm is adding jobs againTCS added 9,279 employees in the April-June quarter after steep headcount declines in late 2025. The shift reflects selective hiring, redeployment and rising demand for AI and digital skills.

Govt eyes stronger forex reserves; FM likely to meet banks soonThe government's move comes at a time when India's foreign exchange reserves have declined sharply. The reserves fell from around $728 billion in February to nearly $682 billion by the end of May.

Home loan on a rented house? Here's what you can still claim under new tax regimeAs taxpayers file their ITRs, understanding home loan tax rules has become more important than ever. In this edition of 'KNOW YOUR TAX', we explain what you can still claim on a rented property under the new tax regime.

TCS, Infosys, HCLTech rise up to 4%: Why are IT stocks rising today?TCS's stronger-than-expected June-quarter results pushed IT stocks higher on Friday. The Nifty IT index climbed 1.82%, while the BSE Focused IT index gained nearly 2%.

entertainment.headline: Naseeruddin Shah called Anupam Kher a clown. Video resurfaces amid Ram Temple row An old video of Naseeruddin Shah calling Anupam Kher a clown has resurfaced after Kher's remarks on the alleged theft of donations at the Ayodhya Ram Temple.

Lock Upp: Genelia Deshmukh reacts as Ram Kapoor reveals childhood trauma Actor Genelia Deshmukh reacted after television star Ram Kapoor revealed he was molested at 13 on Lock Upp: Sach Ya Sazaa. She praised his courage and said his emotional confession left her in tears.

Mahesh Manjrekar's wife Medha, 59, reveals cancer diagnosis; shares gratitude post Actor Medha Manjrekar, wife of filmmaker-actor Mahesh Manjrekar, has revealed that she has been diagnosed with cancer. In a heartfelt Instagram post, she reflected on her treatment journey, saying faith and the unwavering support of her loved ones helped her through the difficult phase.

Rashmika calls Ranabaali look extremely scary; Vijay Deverakonda corrects her Rashmika Mandanna shared a blurred still from Ranabaali and called what the team was making looked extremely scary, before Vijay Deverakonda disagreed with her. Their exchange has sharpened interest in his avatar and in one of the film's key sequences.

Tamil actor Arya named in Rs 1.8 crore cheating case by equipment rental firm A Hyderabad camera rental company has accused actor-producer Arya, producer Vinod and others of failing to clear over Rs 1.8 crore in dues from Ananthan Kaadu. Police have registered a case for cheating and criminal intimidation and are examining the submitted documents.

Sir Sam Neill, Jurassic Park star, dies at 78 Veteran New Zealand actor Sam Neill, famed for his role as Dr Alan Grant in the Jurassic Park series, died peacefully at 78 in Sydney, surrounded by family. Despite battling a rare form of blood cancer, he remained cancer-free at his time of death.

Maa Inti Bangaaram on OTT: When and where to watch Samantha's Rs 100 crore film Actor Samantha's Maa Inti Bangaaram will begin streaming this month. The digital release follows a Rs 100-crore theatrical run and renewed discussion around female-led cinema. Read on to find out when and where to watch the film.

Michael crosses $1 billion after 12 weekends in theatres, a first for Lionsgate Michael has crossed $1 billion worldwide after 12 weekends in theatres, becoming Lionsgate's first film to reach the mark. The milestone also makes it the studio's biggest title and delivers career-best totals for Antoine Fuqua and Graham King.

Pawan Kalyan recovering after shoulder surgery, CM Chandrababu Naidu visits him Actor and Andhra Pradesh Deputy CM Pawan Kalyan is recovering in a Mumbai hospital after right shoulder surgery. CM Chandrababu Naidu's visited him over the weekend.

I know what I'm doing: Kalki director defends film's climax, Karna portrayal Filmmaker Nag Ashwin has defended his portrayal of Karna in Kalki 2898 AD after a social media comparison with the newly released film Lenin. His response invoked the Mahabharata and also turned attention to the still-evolving second part.

I don't please everyone: Anupam Kher's cryptic post after Ram Mandir theft remark Anupam Kher shared a video after criticism over his remarks on the Ram Mandir donation theft in Ayodhya. In an X video on Sunday, the actor said he speaks fearlessly because he does not try to please everyone.

Rama, Dada, Akbar or Azhar: Why Bollywood keeps getting biopic casting wrong Actors in Bollywood biographical roles almost never resemble the real people they portray. The issue has become the defining characteristic of the Hindi biopic.

Akhil Akkineni's freak accident changed him, says Nagarjuna at Lenin event Actor Nagarjuna revealed that his son Akhil Akkineni lost sensation in his hand after a freak accident nearly two years ago. Speaking at Lenin's success event, he said the difficult phase changed Akhil's outlook and made him calmer.

Yash's Toxic: Women, Tabaahi and a marketing strategy built on provocation? Toxic's Tabaahi song and other proms have triggered an online debate over how its women are presented. The reaction has renewed questions about whether controversy now functions as visibility in film marketing.

Dhamaal 4 box office Day 3: Ajay Devgn's film gets franchise-best opening weekend Dhamaal 4 earned Rs 65 crore nett in India and Rs 92 crore worldwide in its first three days. The Sunday jump and occupancy growth pushed it past earlier franchise openers and slightly ahead of Welcome To The Jungle.

Imtiaz Ali's daughter Ida gets engaged in a picture-perfect Norway moment. Watch Filmmaker Imtiaz Ali's daughter Ida Ali is engaged to longtime boyfriend Krish Agrawal. She shared photos and videos from the dreamy proposal in Norway. Several celebrities congratulated the couple on Instagram.

Kusha Kapila says viral selfie with Samay Raina is AI-generated: Fake and seeded Actor-influencer Kusha Kapila said a viral selfie with stand-up comedian Samay Raina was fake, and her face had been altered using AI. She accused troll networks of using the image to minimise her.

Muzammil Ibrahim reacts to viral no regrets clip on break-up with Deepika Padukone Actor Muzammil Ibrahim has clarified that the Deepika Padukone breakup clip doing the rounds is from an older interview and lacks full context. His note stresses that he spoke honestly but respectfully, even as snippets sparked fresh online chatter.

Salman Khan rocks a rugged cowboy avatar at Bandra studio amid SVC 63 buzz Actor Salman Khan was photographed outside a dubbing studio in Bandra wearing a cowboy-inspired outfit. The appearance has fuelled speculation over his next film with Nayanthara, tentatively titled SVC 63.

general news.headline: Win for Vijay govt as Supreme Court pauses blanket ban on cow slaughter orderThe Supreme Court granted interim relief to the Tamil Nadu government, issued notice on its plea challenging the High Court's directions, and stayed the operation of the order until further proceedings.

Iran hits back after US strikes, claims destruction of Patriot systems, drone fleet  Live Updates In response to fresh American attacks, Iran launched coordinated missile and drone strikes on US-linked sites across the Gulf. The fresh exchange widened the conflict, sharpened shipping tensions and cast doubt on recent ceasefire efforts.

SC seeks Ram Mandir donation theft report, to hear CBI probe plea next weekThe case concerns the theft of cash and valuables donated at the Ram Temple in Ayodhya, with the Supreme Court directing the Special Investigation Team (SIT) to report on its probe's progress.

How an Indian startup is using software to break China's rare earth monopolyHandcrafted ByOur Editors A Bengaluru-based startup is building motors without permanent magnets. By doing so, it is trying to break China's stronghold on rare earth minerals. India Today Tech reports.

India's T20I freefall explained: Did Gambhir and Agarkar fix what wasn't broken?India's disastrous 0-6 tour of Ireland and England has raised questions far bigger than Shreyas Iyer's winless start as T20I captain. In trying to rebuild a world champion side, have Gautam Gambhir and the selection committee dismantled a setup that never really needed fixing?

Thousands of dog-owners had to put pets to death in Punjab; a terrorism horror storyDiljit Dosanjh's Satluj has brought the dangerous days of terrorism in Punjab into the spotlight. One well-documented aspect of those days is the massacre of thousands of dogs across the state. At a time when we are debating how best or kindly to deal with street dogs, it is instructive to read this horror story about why Punjab's dogs became collateral damage.

Why tapped solar electricity is going down the drain in IndiaWhile solar power generation in India has increased manifold, storage capacity lags drastically. That's why electricity generated from the sun is going down the drain. The challenge is to make solar power available when consumers need it the most.

No MCQs, no shortcuts: Why India's Physics Olympiad Gold sweep is historicFive Indian students delivered one of the country's greatest performances in international science by sweeping gold at the International Physics Olympiad 2026. Here's why a former JEE Physics teacher believes this feat deserves far more attention than it has received.

Puja held inside moving train, Railways clarifies after video goes viralA video showing a priest conducting a puja inside a moving train coach went viral online. Northern Railways said the ritual took place in a privately booked saloon car, prompting debate over activities allowed during journeys.

IPL: Stephen Fleming steps down as CSK head coach after 17 years at helmIPL: Chennai Super Kings parted ways with head coach Stephen Fleming after an 18-year association, drawing the curtains on the IPL's most successful coaching era. CSK finished three consecutive seasons outside the top four.

After Trump ally's sudden death, MAGA fires up conspiracy theoriesUS Senator Lindsey Graham's death unleashed a wave of conspiracy theories across MAGA circles, with some influencers alleging Russia or Iran was behind it. His recent Ukraine visit and past threats from Tehran fuelled the speculation, despite authorities saying there was no evidence of foul play.

Decide citizenship via fair process: SC relief for 27 declared foreigners in AssamThe Supreme Court set aside Gauhati High Court orders declaring 27 Assam residents foreigners, directing Foreigners Tribunals to conduct fresh hearings while stressing fairness in citizenship determination.

Rama, Dada, Akbar or Azhar: Why Bollywood keeps getting biopic casting wrongActors in Bollywood biographical roles almost never resemble the real people they portray. The issue has become the defining characteristic of the Hindi biopic.

What's behind India's tougher stance in trade talks with USThe US entered trade talks from a position of strength. But a series of developments may be tilting the balance towards New Delhi. Here's why India is in no hurry to strike a deal.

Bengaluru teacher hangs herself months after love marriage, blames husband in noteShwetha, a teacher at a private school, had a love marriage just four months ago. Her body was found hanging from a ceiling fan.

Why TCS, Infosys and HCLTech shares rose despite a weak marketShares of Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), Infosys, HCLTech and LTIMindtree rose in early trade, outperforming the broader market and signalling renewed investor interest in a sector that has been under pressure for months.

Terrifying moment bison chases tourist, tosses him 8ft into the air at US parkA bull bison chased a tourist and tossed him into the air at Yellowstone National Park's Bridge Bay Campground in the US state of Wyoming. The terrifying encounter has gone viral on social media.

Sir Sam Neill, Jurassic Park star, dies at 78Veteran New Zealand actor Sam Neill, famed for his role as Dr Alan Grant in the Jurassic Park series, died peacefully at 78 in Sydney, surrounded by family. Despite battling a rare form of blood cancer, he remained cancer-free at his time of death.

Has CJP's dancing Dahiya failed to impress a fasting Sonam Wangchuk?Vijeta Dahiya's dance videos from the Cockroach Janta Party's protest stage at New Delhi's Jantar Mantar, right next to a fasting Sonam Wangchuk, have gone viral. Dahiya says he wants to be the world's first dancing activist. Here's what Dahiya has to say about his dancing and Wangchuk's reaction.

Fire, toxic smoke, blocked exits: How Bangkok pub turned into a death trapA fire tore through Bangkok's Rong Beer Na Lat Phrao pub around midnight, killing 27 people. Officials believe many people were trapped inside due to obstructed emergency exits.

Why does Delhi feel unbearably hot without a heatwave?Delhi is experiencing oppressive humidity during a break in the southwest monsoon. The moisture-heavy air is pushing the apparent temperature far above the actual reading.

Meet 12-year-old Mana Jampala who built an AI startup with clients in 3 countriesMana Jampala is only 12, but she is already running an AI startup with deployments in Canada, India and Cambodia. After learning Python at nine and building AI products by 11, the Grade 7 student has developed AI-powered tools that help businesses automate customer calls and everyday operations.

After 20,000 layoffs, TCS is building a team of 8,900 forward deployed engineersTCS is going through a period of transformation. After laying off 20,000 workers, the company plans to bet big on AI with 8,900 forward deployed engineers (FDEs). Here is all you need to know.

Suvendu to replace Mamata's 'Khela Hobey Divas' with 'Ayushman Divas'Suvendu Adhikari replacing Mamata Banerjee's 'Khela Hobey Divas' with 'Ayushman Divas' marks a symbolic shift that also coincides with the BJP government's completion of 100 days in office.

sports.headline: Shreyas Iyer's interview over Sooryavanshi snub irks ex-captain: Not the way to talk Kris Srikkanth criticised Shreyas Iyer and the team management for dropping Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in Southampton T20I. He said the move and Iyer's remarks showed poor handling of a young player.

IPL: Stephen Fleming steps down as CSK head coach after 17 years at helm IPL: Chennai Super Kings parted ways with head coach Stephen Fleming after an 18-year association, drawing the curtains on the IPL's most successful coaching era. CSK finished three consecutive seasons outside the top four.

India's T20I freefall explained: Did Gambhir and Agarkar fix what wasn't broken? India's disastrous 0-6 tour of Ireland and England has raised questions far bigger than Shreyas Iyer's winless start as T20I captain. In trying to rebuild a world champion side, have Gautam Gambhir and the selection committee dismantled a setup that never really needed fixing?

Andy Flower took England to No.1: Nasser backs RCB coach to replace McCullum Former England captain Nasser Hussain has backed Andy Flower to replace Brendon McCullum as England's Test head coach, saying his meticulous approach is exactly what the team needs after the Bazball era.

Zverev aims to move beyond tennis' third guy tag, close gap on Sinner and Alcaraz Wimbledon runner-up Alexander Zverev will return to world No. 2 on Monday, but believes his attacking game has finally brought him closer to Jannik Sinner and Carlos Alcaraz after years of being labelled tennis' "third guy".

Not at full throttle, Jannik Sinner still stands atop the tennis world Jannik Sinner proved that true champions are not defined only by dominance but by their ability to survive adversity and find a way to win. On Sunday, the Italian showcased exactly that, defending his Wimbledon title with a hard-fought victory over Alexander Zverev in the final.

Yastika Bhatia reveals how Mandhana's advice fuelled her Lord's comeback century INDW vs ENGW, one-off Test: Yastika Bhatia credited Smriti Mandhana's advice for helping her overcome a serious knee injury after marking her comeback with a maiden international century at Lord's.

The Bazball verdict: Brilliant at home, exposed by the elite Brendon McCullum's Bazball promised to revolutionise England's Test cricket. Four years later, sacked after a humiliating Ashes defeat, the numbers reveal the truth: it worked at home and against weak teams. Against India and Australia, it crumbled.

Lord's Test: Yastika Bhatia, Kranti Gaud leave India on verge of famous win at Day 3 Yastika Bhatia became the first woman to score a Test hundred at Lord's before Kranti Gaud's fiery spell left England six down as India took complete control on Day 3 of the historic Women's Test, setting the hosts a daunting 457-run target.

Alexander Zverev's runner-up finish fuels belief he can one day win Wimbledon Alexander Zverev said reaching his maiden Wimbledon final has convinced him he can one day lift the title despite losing to Jannik Sinner. The German praised the world No. 1 while reflecting on a breakthrough fortnight at the All England Club.

Ice-cool Jannik Sinner fights off feisty Alexander Zverev to retain crown World No.1 Jannik Sinner came from a set down to beat Alexander Zverev 6-7(7), 7-6(2), 6-3, 6-4 and successfully defend his Wimbledon title on Sunday, July 12 on Centre Court.

Rahul Dravid among candidates to replace Brendon McCullum as England Test coachRahul Dravid has reportedly emerged as a contender to replace Brendon McCullum as England's Test coach. The former India coach features on a shortlist that includes Andy Flower and Richard Dawson, though the report says he is reluctant to coach full-time.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi makes Wimbledon debut, picks favourite tennis starTeen cricket sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi enjoyed his first Wimbledon visit, attending the men's singles final alongside Yuvraj Singh, Abhishek Sharma and Sanju Samson. The 15-year-old also revealed his admiration for Novak Djokovic and backed Jannik Sinner.

Wimbledon Highlights: Sinner crowned champion again, beat Zverev in finalWimbledon Final Highlights: Jannik Sinner has successfully defended his Wimbledon crown after beating Alexander Zverev 6-7(7), 7-6(2), 6-3, 6-4 in three hours and 46 minutes on Centre Court.

End of Bazball as McCullum sacked as Test coach after Ben Stokes retirementBrendon McCullum has been sacked as England's Test head coach, just days after Ben Stokes announced his retirement from international cricket. However, the former New Zealand captain will continue in his role as head coach of England's ODI and T20I sides.

Yastika Bhatia becomes first woman to hit Test century at Lord'sEngland vs India, only Test: Yastika Bhatia became the first batter to score a century in a women's Test at Lord's Cricket Ground as India dominated proceedings on Day 3 of the iconic match.

FIFA will explore expanding World Cup to 64 teams before 2030 edition: InfantinoFIFA president confirmed discussions for a 64-team tournament will take place ahead of the 2030 centenary edition, insisting football's global showpiece must look beyond the traditional dominance of Europe and South America.

India tour of England, Zimbabwe: Prince and Bishnoi named injury replacementsIndia have brought in Prince Yadav and Ravi Bishnoi after Harshit Rana and Varun Chakaravarthy suffered hamstring injuries during the recently-concluded England T20Is. The reshuffle comes ahead of the ODIs in England and the T20Is in Zimbabwe.

Is cricket's DRS better than football's VAR?Football and cricket embraced video reviews to eliminate costly mistakes. But one sport gave players the power to challenge decisions, while the other left it to officials, shaping every debate around VAR and DRS since.

Lionel Scaloni dismisses Argentina-England history before World Cup semi-finalFIFA World Cup: Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni played down his country's history with England, insisting that the semi-final was "just a football game" after his side defeated Switzerland 3-1 in the quarter-finals.

IPL puts heavy make-up on batters: Sanjay Manjrekar on India's T20I debacleFormer India batter Sanjay Manjrekar believes India's disappointing T20I tour should shift the focus from the players to the IPL, saying the tournament's batting-friendly conditions do not adequately prepare batters for the demands of overseas cricket.

Preach patience, practice panic: How India failed Sooryavanshi and Samson in the UKIndia's 0-6 tour of the United Kingdom will be remembered for the records it shattered, but perhaps its biggest failure lay elsewhere. In the space of one disastrous series, India risked damaging the confidence of two batters at opposite ends of their careers, raising uncomfortable questions about the team's handling of Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Sanju Samson.

Argentina bias at FIFA World Cup? Swiss coach slams unacceptable Embolo red cardSwitzerland coach Murat Yakin blasted the rule behind Breel Embolo's controversial red card as "unacceptable" after Argentina scored twice in extra time to seal a 3-1 win and end the Swiss fairytale in the FIFA World Cup quarter-finals on Saturday.

Messi's heated exchange with Portuguese referee goes viral: Don't be disrespectfulLionel Messi found himself at the center of another viral World Cup moment, this time after a heated exchange with Portuguese referee João Pinheiro during Argentina's dramatic 3-1 extra-time win over Switzerland.

A freer Zverev, an unstoppable Sinner. Wimbledon final promises blockbusterWimbledon 2026: Fresh off his maiden Grand Slam triumph at Roland Garros, Alexander Zverev faces his toughest test yet as world No.1 Jannik Sinner stands between him and a second straight major title.

Semifinals time: England vs Argentina, Spain vs France promise epic showdownsEngland will face Argentina and Spain will meet France in the 2026 FIFA World Cup semi-finals. The last-four line-up keeps alive a potential Messi-Mbappe final rematch from Qatar.

Messi's streak ends, but Argentina scrape into semis to keep World Cup dream aliveFIFA World Cup: Lionel Messi could not deliver another rescue act, but Argentina found a new hero. Julián Álvarez's stunning extra-time strike inspired a 3-1 win over 10-man Switzerland to send the defending champions into the semi-finals and keep their title defence alive.

Conor McGregor's comeback ends in injury heartbreak: What happened at UFC 329Conor McGregor's UFC 329 comeback ended 69 seconds in after a leg injury against Max Holloway. The stoppage deepened doubts over his future and handed Holloway a technical knockout win.

Breel Embolo devastated after costly blunder vs Argentina ends his World Cup dreamFIFA World Cup: Breel Embolo was left in tears after the Switzerland striker was sent off for simulation during his side's quarter-final against Argentina on Saturday.

Erling Haaland's father blasts referee after Norway's World Cup exit to EnglandAlf-Inge Haaland, the father of Norway striker Erling Haaland, criticised the referee after England's 2-1 extra-time win over Norway in the FIFA World Cup quarter-final on Saturday.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
